reviews.review_description: ATTENTION! We paid for unlimited access and did not receive paid content! We have already written three letters to official support and have NOT received a single response within a month! None at all. Horrible support. And this is a direct deception of users.

reviews.review_title: Paid Premium but can only view the free
reviews.review_description: I try to access the premium content that I paid a subscription for, but I had a notification flash up on my headset when I clicked the paid content, along the lines of ‘we are loading this for you’. But despite paying for it I can only access the free stuff. It’s a shame because it looks amazing and is the kind of calm I need.

I tried this meditation app on a brief free trial. I already have most of the meditation apps that are available on Meta Quest. 
The environments are very pretty and relaxing. It basically has the same elements of Nature Treks. I liked the interaction with the animals but when you catch a bird in the forest, it just hangs upside down in front of you and there is no way to let it go! You just have to walk around the forest with a bird hanging in front of your face.
The subscription pricing does not make sense. 
$4 per month but $50 annually? So its cheaper to buy monthly and no discount for an annual membership? Lifetime seems reasonable if there was more content. There is simply not that many environments and content for me to unlock warranting a subscription.
I paid. Why cant I access the hot air Balloons. It says its being prepared? When will it be ready exactly!!??
I paid $3.99 for a month of premium, meta store shows i have an active subscription, but the app says i need to "upgrade" to access additional content.  Once fixed, i will change to 5 stars.

reviews.review_title: You can use this without email or pay
reviews.review_description: I tried the free version which currently has all the features unlocked. The ui is nice, the environments are ok, the fishes and turtles that move towards you look incredible and that's probably my favourite feature. There's a solid foundation but the app has a long way to go, but the dev reached out to me on reddit and I gave them some suggestion. The voice is relaxing and it's simple to use and has a good startup tutorial sequence. Personally to relax in VR and feel mindful I'd rather color in some cubes or do something with my hands rather than sit and stare at a scene that looks less visually impressive than the quest home screens.

Paid $4.99 for first month and it says i only have 45 minutes left in my trial ill increase my rating once rectified

*i think its fixed now and recognizes my paid subscription

I'm not usually someone who meditates, but the visuals were immersive and calming, and the music was beautifully done. I especially loved the ocean experience, when turtles gently swam toward my hand as I held out seaweed with the controller. The app ran smoothly, and I appreciated how easy it was to use. Looking forward to seeing the new experiences when they're released.

For transparency, the developer asked me to try out the game, but I didn't receive any form of payment. They didn't even explicitly ask me to review it. They just asked for thoughts and feedback. I elected to put them in the form of a store page review.

I love they chose animated environments instead of photorealistic ones. I've seen other mindfulness apps go with photorealistic backgrounds, and they can feel static and unimmersive. I've seen other apps use similarly animated environments, but many of them came out years ago and look dated.

In comparison, Visutate's environments are modern, beautifully high definition, clean and colorful. Of the four available environments, I will say enchanted grove was a bit underwhelming. I liked the rest a lot though, especially the lotus pond.

The UI is clean. It reminds me of the Quest's recent UI overhaul, so it'll feel familiar to people used to navigating Horizon OS.

While some features are still in development, the current customization options available are solid. You can adjust music and environmental volume; enable text, visual and/or voice breathing prompts; and choose from a few environmental effects.

For a suggestion, I think some kind of "Are you sure?" prompt would be welcome before ending a breathing exercise. I didn't realize clicking the breathing icon again would end the exercise. I thought it'd let me adjust options mid session.

Also, the user profile page features a seven-day mindfulness program, but I completed today's exercise by popping in and out of the environment without spending the full 10 minutes there. Would be nice to be able to reset my progress, and for the game to more minutely track actual time engaging in an exercise.

For early access, I'm happy giving it a five-star review based on the potential I see and what looks like a pretty clear roadmap to release more content and features. If you've used similar apps in the past and want something newer, this is worth checking out.

reviews.review_title: My go-to app for my relaxation sessions
reviews.review_description: This app multiplies my regular mindfulness exercises with visuals and immersed experience. It takes my sense of presence to the most peaceful sceneries, slowing my pace down, giving me the sense of abundance and comfort. Regular use of this app has improved my contentment and inner peace. The UI and graphics are the top  qualityfor such an app for VR tech. I recommend this 100% to all my friends I care about.

I have been using this for a few days and the experience is even better than I expected. The visuals are nice, the sounds are calming, and the interactions feel really natural. The interface is clean and looks professional, and I have not experienced a single lag or stutter the whole time which feels surprisingly good. Even though the app is still in development, using this app is already super relaxing and fun.

10 bucks a month or 50 a year. Prompted me to set up an account (by giving it an email address) then refused to log me in saying I'm not an administrator or manager. Not a good look to demand my email then provide nothing for allowing you to spam me. 

What little I could see looked OK (graphics-wise) but most all content is behind a paywall.

I deplore subscription models, and what this dev is charging is way too much given it is brand new. If you want early adopters to take a chance on an app like this, offer it as a lifetime purchase at a very moderate price to earn decent reviews. The devs are dreaming of raking in cash via an expensive subscription model. I'm not supporting that type of strategy because it's a rip-off and anti-consumer.  Looking at the app's website suggests this app is primarily marketed to Enterprise customers.  Individual consumers seem to be a total afterthought which is reflected in their anti-consumer pricing strategy.  If you're just a regular person looking for a great meditation app on Quest, there are plenty of options both free & paid.  I *highly* recommend Realms of Flow as the dev keeps adding content without charging existing users.  Flowborne VR and Maloka are free and absolutely fantastic.  Float VR is just wonderful with ongoing development (with a fantastic Group on FB)--although they offer a subscription, at least they offer a lifetime purchase at a moderate price.  And the devs are awesome and interact with users every day on FB.

developer_response.body:
Thank you for being one of our first users and providing such detailed, valuable feedback during early access. Based on your insights, we have executed the following work:

1. Fixed the login/account access issue - There's also now a "skip for now" option for quick access without registration
2. Removed the paywall entirely for all experiences until September 15th - While we normally offer free trial content, early users get complete access
3. Added account deletion option in profile settings for full user control. We never spam users, but this provides additional peace of mind

You tried one of our initial versions and we hope you'll give the significantly improved version another chance. The new version has enhanced graphics, performance, teleportation, and camera view control features, and interactions available for both hand tracking and controllers.

We're committed to continuous development rather than a 'launch and forget' approach, which is why we use a subscription model. However, we understand subscription models aren't for everyone. Your feedback about lifetime pricing options is something we're seriously considering for our early users.

Thank you again for the feedback. We're always listening at hello@visutate.com, and your input truly makes a difference.

reviews.review_title: If boring is calming to you
reviews.review_description:
Than this is a visually pleasing way to be bored. 
Meditation is irritating to me rather than restful *ADHD/AUTISM have something to do with it I suspect*🤷🏼‍♀️ but the visuals are clean and the sounds reflect the environment in a non intrusive way.

The breathing exercises are so varied and keep things interesting with voice guide. Interacting with the creatures around me during a session is such a nice touch. Plus, the UI/UX is friendly, clear, and super easy to navigate.

developer_response.body: Thank you for the 5-star review! Great to hear you're enjoying the breathing exercises and guided audio sessions in Visutate. We're pleased that the interactions and clean design are enhancing your experience.

reviews.review_description: I like its interactivity. Unless standart 360 videos with tiring sounds, there is much to interact with. I can not wait to see more interaction and storytelling experience from the developers

developer_response.body: Thank you for the 5-star review! We appreciate you highlighting what makes Visutate experiences different from standard 360 videos. Great to hear you're enjoying the interactive elements. We have some exciting interactive storytelling features in development.

reviews.review_description: I’ve really enjoyed using this app for meditation and relaxation. The environments are beautifully designed and instantly set the mood for winding down.
Pretty good. It is good to have 10-15 minutes of breathing in my fast paced life.

Found this when I was in need of something to find balance in my everyday life. It's really calming and it just brings a smile. The visuals are magical and the sounds are on spot! I can't wait when you can actually interact with your surroundings. This sounds exciting. Wishing all the best to the team behind this masterpiece!
